title: Adam Schefter: No ‘under-the-table’ A.J. Brown trade in place for Patriots, Eagles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Adam Schefter denies any secret agreement between Patriots and Eagles for A.J. Brown trade.
Trade for A.J. Brown may be postponed until after June 1 due to salary cap issues.
A.J. Brown had 78 catches for 1,003 yards and seven touchdowns in 2025 with the Eagles.

title: This police officer is running Boston to support self-defense training
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Caroline Butts, a Milton police officer, is running her third Boston Marathon in support of The Vanessa Marcotte Foundation.
The foundation focuses on violence prevention, self-defense, and female empowerment against harassment and violence.
Butts aims to use her marathon run to advocate for women's empowerment and spread awareness of violence against women.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 25)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A one-family Cape Cod at 2 Huron Road, Acton sold for $1,325,000 with 4 bedrooms and 5 baths.
A condo at 397-399 Commonwealth Ave., Boston reached a sale price of $6,725,000.
A single-family ranch at 164 Hillside Ave., Arlington sold for $2,165,000 with 2 bedrooms and 1 bath.

title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was convicted for aggravated rape, kidnapping, and armed robbery for crimes committed in 1989.
DNA technology advancements in 2022 helped identify Gale, leading to his capture after a 40-mile chase in Los Angeles.
The victims escaped to safety after being assaulted in a Framingham store; Gale evaded capture for over three decades.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson introduced as Providence head coach, jokingly cited wife as testimony to recruiting skills.
Hodgson previously led South Florida to a 25-9 record and an NCAA tournament bid in 2025-26.
Providence seeks to rebuild after consecutive sub-.500 seasons; Hodgson's recruiting and analytics praised.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alex Castrichini, 37, from Concord, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care.
Castrichini is inspired by stories from the Boston CASA Gala, aiming to raise awareness for foster children's challenges.
Boston CASA trains volunteers as court-appointed advocates; Castrichini joined their board to support foster kids further.

title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Teachers Union protests potential layoff of over 400 school staff due to proposed budget cuts.
Proposed staffing cuts would heavily impact multilingual learners and students with special education needs.
Boston's School Committee to vote on a $1.7 billion budget amid protests for additional funding.

title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 160 'No Kings' protests are scheduled on March 28 across Massachusetts, part of a larger national mobilization.
'No Kings' protests focus on democracy and opposition to Trump's policies, including immigration and U.S. involvement in Iran.
Massachusetts ranks second after California for the number of 'No Kings' events, reflecting widespread local engagement.

title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is a leading NBA MVP candidate, praised for his leadership during Jayson Tatum's injury absence.
ESPN's 'Pardon The Interruption' hosts, Michael Wilbon and Tony Kornheiser, back Brown as the MVP frontrunner.
Brown averages career highs in points, rebounds, and assists, helping the Celtics to second place in the East.

title: Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Marshfield police are searching for 8-year-old Callahan Pierce, last seen near 236 Moraine St. around 7 p.m. Tuesday.
Residents with home cameras in the area are urged to check footage to aid in locating Callahan Pierce.
Police request the public avoid the search area to prevent hindering infrared cameras and scent dogs.
